items.content_text:
We always enjoy getting out into the community! Recently, our team had the pleasure of stopping by the Chattanooga Breakfast Rotary Club to talk all things MBEC. 🗣️
 There can be a lot of mystery (and sometimes misinformation) surrounding wastewater processes, but we believe in total transparency. Our administrators genuinely enjoy geeking out over how we keep our local environment clean, and we are an open book! 🔬♻️
 Got a question? Want to bust a wastewater myth? Need to schedule a media interview? We are here for it. Don’t hesitate to reach out or drop a question in the comments (or inboxes)!👇
 #OneChattanooga #MBEC #CHARiverChampions #Chattanooga #Wastewater

items.title: Join us in welcoming Bright Family Dentistry to #EastRidgeTN! 🦷  The City of East Ridge was proud to celebrate the gran...
items.content_text:
Join us in welcoming Bright Family Dentistry to #EastRidgeTN! 🦷 
 The City of East Ridge was proud to celebrate the grand opening and ribbon cutting of Bright Family Dentistry on Thursday, July 9, as we officially welcomed one of our newest healthcare providers to the community network. ✂️👏🏼
 Led by Dr. Robina Habib, Bright Family Dentistry offers comprehensive dental care for patients of all ages, including preventive, restorative, cosmetic, and family dentistry services. With more than 20 years of clinical experience, Dr. Habib and her team are committed to providing personalized, compassionate care in a welcoming environment.
 📍1421 Armour Street, East Ridge, TN 
 A special thank you to the East Ridge Council of the Chattanooga Chamber of Commerce, community partners, elected officials, and everyone who joined us in celebrating this exciting milestone. 
 We are excited to have them as part of the East Ridge business community and wish them many years of success serving our residents and families! 🤍

items.title: 🌟 Above & Beyond the Call of Duty! 🌟 We want to give a shoutout and a well-deserved congratulations to two of our incr...
items.content_text:
🌟 Above & Beyond the Call of Duty! 🌟
 We want to give a shoutout and a well-deserved congratulations to two of our incredible team members, Marcie and Nicole!
 Recently, a mother riding a bicycle with her small child in a bike seat stopped by the Outdoor Chattanooga building. They were stranded and desperately needed an urgent bike repair.
 Without a second thought, Marcie and Nicole jumped right into action. 🛠️✨
 In less than 10 minutes, the bike was fixed, the safety checks were done, and this sweet family was safely back on their way!
 This kind of immediate, helpful, and compassionate response is exactly what makes our Parks and Outdoors Department so special. We don’t just care about the parks and trails—we care about the community members who use them.
 Thank you, Marcie and Nicole, for your quick thinking, expert skills, and for always putting our community first!
 Drop a 👏 in the comments to help us congratulate them!
 #OutdoorChattanooga #Chattanooga #CommunityFirst #GoingAboveAndBeyond #cityinapark

items.title: Thanks to funding through the National Endowment for the Arts, we have been able to support 11 projects through the Arts...
items.content_text:
Thanks to funding through the National Endowment for the Arts, we have been able to support 11 projects through the ArtsBuild United We Create: America 250 grant program.
 The United We Create: America 250 grant program is providing project funding to nonprofit organizations partnering with an artist or group of artists to create artworks that elevate, honor, and reflect on the 250th anniversary of the United States. Projects engage residents and visitors in meaningful exploration of our nation’s past, present, and future, with a particular focus on the unique character and stories of Chattanooga and Hamilton County.
 Join us at Plant Bar on Tuesday, 7/14 6:30 pm for Cast Iron Storytelling’s Volunteer: True Stories from TN! Free to attend. 
 To stay up to date with all the events happening from now until the end of this year, sign up for the ArtsBuild newsletter through our website. ArtsBuild.com.
 #nationalendowmentforthearts #America250 #artsareessential #castironstorytelling #nextexitproductions

items.title: Breastfeeding can come with many challenges for a new mom, but it also offers many amazing benefits for you and your bab...
items.content_text:
Breastfeeding can come with many challenges for a new mom, but it also offers many amazing benefits for you and your baby.
 Breastfeeding is very convenient, as the milk is always warm and ready when your baby is hungry. 
 Breastfeeding helps you to save on other health and nutrition-related costs. It is very easy for babies to digest, as it is packed with essential nutrients and vitamins for strong, healthy growth. Plus, it lowers their overall risk of some illnesses, such as SIDS. 
 Need help getting started? WIC offers breastfeeding peer counselors to assist you in your breastfeeding journey. For more information regarding WIC, its programs, or your eligibility, please visit Health.HamiltonTN.org or call 423-209-8050.

This is an excellent illustration of environmental stewardship and civic commitment.  Our emergency services crew, led b...
items.content_text: This is an excellent illustration of environmental stewardship and civic commitment.  Our emergency services crew, led by Sherman Sales, has responded to the several trees that have fallen in our neighborhood due to the recent storms.  Our team not only cleared fallen debris from property and roads, but they also saved a honey bee colony, one of nature's most important pollinators.  In order to securely remove and relocate the active hive, Sherman contacted a local honey bee expert and instructed workers to set the tree aside right away. Thousands of honey bees were saved and given a new home where they can thrive because of his quick thinking and leadership.  Thank you to our fantastic crew! Pic and information provided by David Collins.

items.title: Thank you, South Arts!Behind every grant is a story. In Tennessee, South Arts invested more than $613K last year in gran...
items.content_text:
Thank you, South Arts!
Behind every grant is a story.
 In Tennessee, South Arts invested more than $613K last year in grant support, helping 29 arts organizations and 10 individual artists create, connect, and strengthen their communities.
 One of those artists is Tabitha Arnold, Tennessee Fellow and 2025 Southern Prize Winner. Through labor-intensive textile works, she illuminates the often untold history of labor organizing and working-class life in the American South.
 When we invest in artists, we invest in the stories that define our region.
 🎥 Hear from Tate about how you can help fuel the future of Southern artistry through our current grassroots campaign and champion your state to the finish line! (Link in our bio)
 #artists #grants

Countries are increasingly working together to tackle cross-border bribery. This shift is helping to reshape the enforce...
items.content_text:
Countries are increasingly working together to tackle cross-border bribery. This shift is helping to reshape the enforcement of anti-bribery laws worldwide.
 Multijurisdictional resolutions – where two or more countries co-ordinate their investigations and sanctions to address the same bribery scheme – are strengthening global anti-corruption efforts and helping to create a more level playing field for business.
 Authorities are increasingly co-ordinating investigations, aligning sanctions and sharing monetary recoveries across borders.
 Key insights from our latest blog post:
 ➡️ An estimated USD 33.7 billion in sanctions have been imposed through co-ordinated cases
 ➡️ An estimated USD 12.6 billion has been returned to affected jurisdictions whose officials were bribed
 This trend builds on the foundations of the OECD Anti-Bribery Convention, which has set the global standard for combating transnational corruption since 1999.
 Multijurisdictional resolutions offer a path to reshaping how the OECD Anti-Bribery Convention is implemented, replacing isolated national efforts with collaborative enforcement.
 Read the blog post to learn more: https://brnw.ch/21x45Q7
 #OECDintegrity

items.title: 🏡✨ Community Resource Hubs are coming! ✨🏡 Last week, I had the pleasure of bringing the welcome and attending the Comm...
items.content_text:
🏡✨ Community Resource Hubs are coming! ✨🏡
 Last week, I had the pleasure of bringing the welcome and attending the Community Resource Hub Partnership Breakfast at the East Lake Community Center. It was exciting to learn more about this long-awaited initiative and hear how community partners can join the City of Chattanooga in delivering programming directly within our neighborhoods.
 Community Resource Hubs are different by design—transforming our community centers into vibrant neighborhood anchors where residents can access resources, build relationships, and thrive.
 Each of the six hubs will provide coordinated programming and services in four key areas:
 💚 Health & Wellness
 📚 Education & Workforce Development
 🤝 Community Services & Social Support
 🎉 Recreation & Quality of Life
 These hubs will serve as trusted entry points to city services, centers for prevention, and spaces where meaningful partnerships create lasting impact for our communities.
 As the Chair of Community Development and Councilwoman for District 7, I'm excited to see this vision take shape and look forward to the East Lake Community Center becoming a place where opportunity, connection, and support are available closer to home.
 Thank you to everyone working to make this initiative a reality. Together, we're building stronger, healthier, and more connected neighborhoods. 💙
 📸Photos courtesy of Chris Bown
 #District7 #BuildingStrongerCommunities #CommunityResourceHubs #Chattanooga #CommunityPartnership #Health #Education #WorkforceDevelopment #CommunitySupport

items.title: This week marks the 10th residence week of the Urban95 Academy and ten city teams from across the globe are joining us in London f...
items.content_text:
This week marks the 10th residence week of the Urban95 Academy and ten city teams from across the globe are joining us in London for a week of learning engaging and sharing:
 
City of Canning, Australia
Brasilia, Brazil
Envigado, Colombia
City of Oshawa, Canada
London Borough of Camden, UK
Ndola City Council, Zambia
Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina
1803 Fund, Portland, Oregon, US
Greater London Authority, UK
 
Each team has arrived in London with an urgent, place-specific challenge at the intersection of urban design and early childhood development to address: 
 
- How to develop the city centre to be livable for families
- Ways of bringing more access to nature into school yards
- Policies and programmes to improve behavioural and emotional development in children
- Delivering safe routes for strollers
- Achieving safe and accessible doorstep play near housing
- Ensuring high-density areas allow for all children to have access to services
- Closing the learning and achievement gap for low-income children
- Expand access to and increase the use of child‑friendly public spaces among families with young children
- Strategies to mitigate impact of extreme heat on children in cities
- Strategies to support caregivers who bring children to work in market stalls where there is a lack of affordable childcare 
 
What do they all have in common? The recognition that the urban environments we design have the potential to shape the first years of life.

Stay tuned for more insights from U95 10th Residence week!

Savvas Verdis Katie Beck Andrea Torres Sansotta Sam Sternin Van Leer Foundation Aline da Nóbrega Oliveira Daienne Amaral Machado Sebastián Ramírez-Balthazar Salisha Price Erika Kohek, RPP, MCIP Tom Goodeve, MCIP, RPP Rebecca Mushota GEORGE MULENGA Mirela Dzehverović Nermina Suljević Predrag Puharic Elif Esra ONAL Nele Johnson Graeme Bride Patrick Hall Clodagh McCallig Tory Campbell D'Artagnan Caliman (he/him) Camille Idedevbo Shona Scales Vaggy Georgali Betty Owoo Marina Arilha Silva Thiago Ferrari

items.title: Real wages have been growing, but at a slower pace than a year ago. Annual real wage growth was 2.2% in Q1 2026 on avera...
items.content_text:
Real wages have been growing, but at a slower pace than a year ago.
 Annual real wage growth was 2.2% in Q1 2026 on average across countries, compared with 2.7% in Q1 2025.
 Yet even before the recent energy price surge, real wages in several countries had not fully recovered from the 2022 inflation spike.
 Learn more in OECD #EmploymentOutlook: https://brnw.ch/21x45fj

items.title: Advancing the Field of City Leadership: Bloomberg Harvard City Leadership Initiative Welcomes Tenth Class of 46 Mayors from 15 Countries
items.content_text: This week, together with Bloomberg Philanthropies, the Bloomberg Harvard City Leadership Initiative welcomed its tenth class, bringing together 46 mayors from 15 countries who collectively serve more than 22 million residents. Through the nine-month professional management program, the mayors—alongside two top officials from each of their city halls who will begin in August—will gain strategies to improve how local government works and move residents’ chief priorities forward.

items.title: Our Amazing Signal Mountain Fire DepartmentYesterday, the Signal Mountain Fire Department had the opportunity to train a...
items.content_text:
Our Amazing Signal Mountain Fire Department
Yesterday, the Signal Mountain Fire Department had the opportunity to train alongside the Hamilton County Cave & Cliff Team at Signal Point Park, an area where our crews frequently respond to. 
 While this was their training event, our firefighters worked side by side with their team, sharing knowledge and experience in patient extrication from some of the most challenging terrain in our community. Together, we practiced multiple extrication routes and techniques to move injured patients safely and efficiently through the woods.
 Training together strengthens our partnerships, sharpens our skills, and ensures we’re prepared to provide the best possible care when every second counts. Thank you to the Hamilton County Cave & Cliff Team for the invitation and the opportunity to learn together!

items.title: Pedestrian Hit and Killed on Lee Highway CHATTANOOGA, Tenn. -- The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) is investigating...
items.content_text:
Pedestrian Hit and Killed on Lee Highway
 CHATTANOOGA, Tenn. -- The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) is investigating a crash that left a pedestrian dead. 
 CPD's Traffic Unit responded to the 6200 block of Lee Highway shortly after 11:00 Saturday night. The preliminary investigation indicates the pedestrian, a 38-year-old male, was crossing Lee Highway when a vehicle traveling East hit him. The driver stopped and remained at the scene. Hamilton County EMS transported the victim to the hospital where he was pronounced dead. 
 This information is preliminary and is subject to change as the investigation continues. Chattanooga Police ask anyone with information regarding this incident to call 423-698-2525.
 #CPDnews

items.title: When we talk about low voter turnout, it's easy to assume people just don't care. But for many Tennesseans, the path to...
items.content_text:
When we talk about low voter turnout, it's easy to assume people just don't care.
 But for many Tennesseans, the path to the ballot box is filled with barriers—from confusing election rules and lack of transportation to language access, complicated rights restoration, and simply not having the information they need.
 The good news? These are barriers we can help remove together.
 One of the simplest ways to strengthen our democracy is to make sure the people around you have the information and support they need to vote.
 🚶 Join our Walk YOUR Block campaign and commit to talking with your neighbors about the upcoming election. Sign up now: https://secure.everyaction.com/_HTKtYWSokGaL3_iLFN_ug2

items.title: Clearing the backlog to keep East Ridge looking great ! With the annual spring yard cleanup, recent storms and some equi...
items.content_text:
Clearing the backlog to keep East Ridge looking great !
 With the annual spring yard cleanup, recent storms and some equipment issues, brush pick up in East Ridge has fallen behind.  We see it and we're on it.  
 Part of our Sanitation team provides regular curbside trash / recycling service while another part works on bulky item and brush collection – but to get caught up, we've put everyone available on this task.  We will be working extra evenings and weekends over the next two weeks to get things cleared – and if necessary, we have an outside partner ready to assist as well.  
 As a community, we expect our neighbors to keep properties well maintained and looking great – and we need to be sure that we at the City are doing our part to assist in that effort.  Today and every day, we're here to serve.  
 Thanks for your patience and keeping the community great here on the Ridge !

🚨 LAKESIDE LIVE UPDATE: Rubiks Groove Postponed to Friday, July 24 🚨

 Mark your calendars, tell your crew, pack your lawn chairs, and we'll see you Lakeside on Friday, July 24! 🌅🎶
 #LakesideLive #ChesterFrostPark #RubiksGroove #HamiltonCountyParks

 Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office Deputies responded to assist and initiated a traffic stop; however, the suspect fled, resulting in a pursuit. 
 The suspect fled into Georgia and was traveling down Battlefield Parkway when an HCSO deputy conducted a successful PIT maneuver, ending the pursuit.
 This is still an active law enforcement investigation, and charges are pending its outcome.  
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: This evening, Red Bank firefighters make a quick stop on an apartment fire. At 6:15 pm, a 9-1-1 call was made reporting...
items.content_text:
This evening, Red Bank firefighters make a quick stop on an apartment fire. At 6:15 pm, a 9-1-1 call was made reporting a kitchen fire in an apartment. The 9-1-1 dispatcher instructed the caller and others to evacuate the apartment immediately. Red Bank Fire Department responded to the 700 block of Lullwater Road and arrived on the scene reporting smoke pouring out the front of the apartment. Firefighters quickly established water supply, entered the apartment and quickly extinguished the fire in the kitchen. No injuries were reported. 
 Deputy Chief Eddie Iles, Red Bank Fire Department, reported the cause of the fire was accidental cooking. Damage to the apartment is undetermined. However, firefighters contained the fire to the kitchen, preventing it from spreading throughout the apartment. American Red Cross was requested to the scene to assist 1 adult and 3 children with their emergency needs.

items.title: Looks like we have another stormy weekend on the way. Our crews are ready to handle any cleanup, but we could really use...
items.content_text:
Looks like we have another stormy weekend on the way. Our crews are ready to handle any cleanup, but we could really use your help!
 Lately, we’ve been spending more time trying to find downed trees on our trails than it takes to remove them. When a report just says "tree down in the park," our team has to hike around searching for it. That extra search time delays the cleanup and keeps the trails blocked longer for everyone.
 If you spot a fallen tree, please submit it to 311 with the app available in your phone's app store. In your report, please try to be as specific as possible. It makes a massive difference if you can include:
 + A photo of the tree and the path
 + The exact trail name, park, or closest intersection
 + A screenshot of your phone's map app with your location pinned
 The more details we have, the faster we can get out there with the chainsaws and clear the path. Thanks for looking out!

items.title: It’s official: The new path to the Stringer’s Ridge Overlook is OPEN! This week we  hosted a ribbon cutting to celebrate...
items.content_text:
It’s official: The new path to the Stringer’s Ridge Overlook is OPEN!
 This week we  hosted a ribbon cutting to celebrate the completion of Phase One paving! We were honored to walk from the bottom of the Cherokee Trail up to the overlook with Councilwoman Jenny Hill, our incredible Parks Staff, and athletes Joel and Abigail from our Therapeutic Recreation program.
 Thanks to the Tennessee Department of Health's Project Diabetes Grant, this new concrete path makes one of Chattanooga’s favorite outdoor assets safe and accessible. True community health starts with improving the environments where we live, work, and play! Come out and enjoy the view!
 This project is funded under a grant contract with the state of Tennessee.

items.title: Victim and survivor experiences consistently show that leaving an abusive relationship is often not a single decision, b...
items.content_text:
Victim and survivor experiences consistently show that leaving an abusive relationship is often not a single decision, but a process that requires careful planning, resources and support.
 By changing the question from "Why didn't they leave?" to "What barriers are they facing?" we can foster greater understanding and create more survivor-centered responses.
 Everyone deserves safety, support, and the ability to make decisions that are right for their unique circumstances.
 50 Obstacles to Leaving: https://bit.ly/3BNIE2i

items.title: How can the early childhood agenda be advanced in public policy?   Join us for a public lecture exploring how local governments ha...
items.content_text:
How can the early childhood agenda be advanced in public policy?
 
Join us for a public lecture exploring how local governments have been successful in prioritising children in their design and policy. 
 
Speakers include municipal leaders and influential practitioners who have made improving the lives of children central to their work.
 We will hear from: 
- Thiago Ferrari, Municipal Secretary for Early Childhood Development, City of Campinas, Brazil
 - Counsilor Ruari Kelly , Glasgow City Council
 - Mayor Claudio Castro Salas, Renca, Chile
 - Dinah Bornat, architect, author and co-founder of ZCD Architects, Mayors design advocate
 
The event will be chaired by Andrea Torres Sansotta, Director of Programme Support and learning at the Van Leer Foundation
 
📅 Thursday 16th July 
🕟18:30-19:30 BST Time 
📍 In person at LSE Campus: Room MAR.1.04 
🥂 Drinks reception from 18:00 and after the event
💻 Also available online (with interpretation in Spanish and Portuguese). Link to register in due course

Katie Beck Victoria Chavez Van Leer Foundation Rushda Majeed Andrea Torres Sansotta Valentina Riverso

items.title: We have worked tirelessly to reconnect our local trade unions with Hamilton County Schools to create pre-apprenticeship...
items.content_text:
We have worked tirelessly to reconnect our local trade unions with Hamilton County Schools to create pre-apprenticeship pathways for high school students. Yesterday, alongside IBEW and Local 43 leaders, we announced that our county has received a historic investment from Bloomberg Philanthropies to make this a reality.
 These pre-apprenticeships will be available at all three Future Ready Centers, making pathways directly to the Skilled Trades available to every student in Hamilton County.
 Credit goes to countless leaders in our community for making this happen. Kenny Smith has led the charge for decades to bring back vocational education. My friend Jim Bailey made sure Local 43 was at the table. The AGC showed up when we needed them. Chattanooga 2.0 pulled everyone together. The city, county, and school system were aligned.
 Very grateful that everyone in our county is pulling in the same direction to provide career pathways for every student.

items.content_text:
🚨 New Look. Same Commitment to Transparency.
 The City of Red Bank has upgraded its public notice signs to make it easier than ever to stay informed about local government meetings and public notices.
 🎨      Color-coded signs identify each board and commission at a glance.
 📱    QR codes on every sign provide instant access to meeting agendas, staff reports, and other important information on the City's website.
 When you see one of our new public notice signs, simply scan the QR code with your smartphone to learn more about the upcoming meeting or public hearing or give us a call at City Hall for more information.
 We're committed to making local government more accessible, transparent, and convenient for everyone.
 Stay informed. Stay engaged. Help shape the future of Red Bank!
 #commuitydevelopment#redbanktn

items.content_text:
Energy inflation in the #OECD continued to rise in May, reaching 15.8% year-on-year, up from 13.2% in April.
 Energy inflation increased in 26 & declined in 11.
 Canada, Lithuania, Türkiye & the United States recorded energy inflation above 20%.
 By contrast, Costa Rica, Denmark, Iceland, Japan and Norway recorded negative energy inflation.
 Learn more: https://brnw.ch/21x4202
 #OECDstats

items.title: What a great afternoon with our friends at EPB 🩸 Today, we had the opportunity to join their Lunch & Learn to talk abou...
items.content_text:
What a great afternoon with our friends at EPB 🩸
 Today, we had the opportunity to join their Lunch & Learn to talk about how you can become a lifesaver in about 30 minutes through blood donation. We also shared why blood donations are so important for patients living with sickle cell disease and the lifesaving impact they have every day.
 We're incredibly grateful for EPB's continued partnership. Since 2009, they've been hosting Blood Assurance drives and have helped save nearly 6,000 lives in our community. That's the power of showing up for your neighbors.
 Their next blood drive is just one week away, and we'd love to see our community come together once again. Thank you, EPB, for your commitment to helping save local lives, we can't wait to see everyone at the drive!
 #GiveLocalSaveLocal #BloodAssurance #CommunityStrong #SickleCellAwareness #DonateBlood #EPB

items.content_text:
One of our core commitments is “access to the outdoors”. This is a major step. Soon the largest ADA playground in the city will open. And we are not done.
The newly paved section of the Cherokee Trail at Stringer's Ridge is officially open! Thanks to Joel and Abigail, as well as District 2 Councilwoman Jenny Hill and our One Chattanooga team who helped bring this project to fruition. This is just phase one of the three-phase plan, and we are excited to share progress updates with you as we have them. Located at the High Ridge trailhead at
 898 High Ridge Drive. 
 This project is funded under a grant contract with the state of Tennessee.

items.title: 🚨 LAKESIDE LIVE UPDATE: Rubiks Groove Postponed to Friday, July 24 🚨 🌧️ Mother Nature had other plans for Saturday, J...
items.content_text:
🚨 LAKESIDE LIVE UPDATE: Rubiks Groove Postponed to Friday, July 24 🚨
 🌧️ Mother Nature had other plans for Saturday, July 11... but she's not canceling the party—just hitting pause! 🎸🎉
 To make sure everyone gets the full Rubiks Groove experience, we've postponed the concert to Friday, July 24. The band was just as excited to play for the Chester Frost crowd as we were, and they're coming back for the rain date to make it an unforgettable night!
 🎶 Music starts at 7:00 PM
 🛥️ New for the July 24 show: Boating in? Pull up to Chester Frost, tune your radio to 87.9 FM, and enjoy the concert live from the water!
 ☀️ We know forecasts don't always get it exactly right, but decisions like this have to be made ahead of time. We'd much rather give everyone a great concert than gamble with the weather. Thanks for rolling with us!
 Mark your calendars, tell your crew, pack your lawn chairs, and we'll see you Lakeside on Friday, July 24! 🌅🎶
 #LakesideLive #ChesterFrostPark #RubiksGroove #HamiltonCountyParks

items.title: 🚨 Chattanooga’s Newest Firefighters! 🚨  A class of 26 cadets made it through our rigorous six-month fire academy to be...
items.content_text:
🚨 Chattanooga’s Newest Firefighters! 🚨 
 A class of 26 cadets made it through our rigorous six-month fire academy to become the newest members of the Chattanooga Fire Department! They successfully completed Emergency Medical Technician training and learned about all of the different aspects of the fire service from other highly skilled, veteran firefighters. Our Training Division and other CFD personnel taught them about fire department operations, fire attack, fire growth & development, fire suppression, and search & rescue techniques. They’ve also mastered a number of skills like hazardous materials response, vehicle extrication, rope rescues, ventilation, and survival tactics- whether they have to save a fellow downed firefighter or save themselves from a fire should they ever become trapped, disoriented, or separated from their crew.
 This class is ready to serve the citizens of Chattanooga and they will immediately head out into our fire companies to work at our 20 fire stations. These firefighters are the future of our department and we wish them all the best as they embark on their careers. 
 Thanks to Bayside Baptist Church for allowing us to hold graduation there!

items.title: Williams Island is 450 acres and just a few miles down river from downtown Chattanooga. Accessible by boat, you can camp...
items.content_text:
Williams Island is 450 acres and just a few miles down river from downtown Chattanooga.
 Accessible by boat, you can camp on the island as you explore the Tennessee River Gorge. 🛶
 Trivia time:
 🐄 The island has been inhabited for over 12k years and used for agricultural use—it still is today with a working cow farm!
 🐦 This island serves as an important stop for many migratory birds.
 🏕️ single and group camp sites are available for free! You just have to reserve them on Tennessee River Gorge Trust ‘s website.
 #paddle #tennessee #camping #visitchatt #nationalparkcity

items.title: What a great evening celebrating another successful year with the East Ridge Council of the Chattanooga Chamber of Comme...
items.content_text:
What a great evening celebrating another successful year with the East Ridge Council of the Chattanooga Chamber of Commerce in #EastRidgeTN! 🎉
 Thank you to everyone who attended, the sponsors who made the event possible, and Topgolf Chattanooga for hosting such a fun evening. We’re grateful for the Chamber’s continued partnership and commitment to supporting East Ridge’s business community.
 Congratulations to Amy Silvers and Shawnna Skiles on their years of dedicated service to the East Ridge Council Board, and best wishes to the incoming leadership as they continue building on that momentum! 💙 #EastRidgeTN
Last night's end of year celebration was a blast!  Thank you to our host, Topgolf, for providing such a wonderful venue, great food, and lots of fun for the attendees.  We'd also like to thank our sponsors for helping making this event possible:
 ▪️McAllister's Deli
 ▪️The Brothers That Just Do Gutters 
 ▪️FirstBank
 ▪️Lumberjacks Tree Service and Hardwood Center 
 ▪️Brookdale Mount Vernon 
 ▪️ASA Engineering and Consulting 
 ▪️Bailey Manor
 ▪️Time Finder
 ▪️Smoothie King
 ▪️The Georgekutty Family 
 ▪️Pro Logo Depot
 We'd also like to recognize two Board members in particular, as their terms have come to an end.  First our President, Amy Silvers, for her volunteer leadership and passion for connecting businesses and people over the past two years. Next, Shawnna Skiles for five years of dedication and a real "get it done" work ethic that has been invaluable to our mission. You both will be missed!  Congratulations to our Board of Directors and everyone that contributed to another successful year!  We appreciate you. 
 If anyone has pictures of the event to share, please post them in the comments.

items.title: As Chattanooga 2.0 continues to grow and deepen our impact across Hamilton County, we're excited to announce an expansion of Jenni...
items.content_text:
As Chattanooga 2.0 continues to grow and deepen our impact across Hamilton County, we're excited to announce an expansion of Jennifer Andrews' leadership role. Jennifer has been promoted to Chief Operating Officer, where she will help strengthen the organization's operational capacity while continuing to lead Chattanooga 2.0's countywide early childhood strategy.

Jennifer joined Chattanooga 2.0 nine years ago and is our longest-tenured employee. During that time, she has helped build many of the collaborative efforts that have shaped Hamilton County's early childhood landscape, including the growth of Early Matters Chattanooga, the Quality Matters Fund, the Ready, Set, Kindergarten campaign, and neighborhood-based initiatives that support children and families.

In her expanded role, Jennifer will help strengthen Chattanooga 2.0's internal operations and organizational infrastructure while continuing to serve as our lead for early childhood strategy and the primary point of contact for Early Matters and our countywide early childhood partnerships. 

Please join us in congratulating Jennifer on this exciting next chapter!

items.title: Now Hiring in #EastRidgeTN! 🚔 The City of East Ridge is currently accepting applications for the position of Police Off...
items.content_text:
Now Hiring in #EastRidgeTN! 🚔
 The City of East Ridge is currently accepting applications for the position of Police Officer with the East Ridge Police Department.
 Join a department committed to professionalism, integrity, and community service while building a rewarding career protecting and serving the citizens of East Ridge. 
 Qualifications:
 ✔️ Tennessee P.O.S.T. Certification
 ✔️ High School Diploma or GED
 ✔️ Must successfully complete physical, psychological, drug screening, background, written, and oral examinations
 What We Offer:
 👏🏼 Starting salary of $59,454.81 annually
 👏🏼 $10,000 Recruitment & Retention Bonus for P.O.S.T. Certified Officers
 👏🏼 $8,000 Recruitment & Retention Bonus for Entry-Level Officers (No Previous Experience)
 👏🏼 Take-home vehicle, competitive benefits, and retirement pension
 👏🏼 12-hour rotating shifts with every other weekend off
 👏🏼 Specialized unit opportunities including K-9, SWAT, Traffic, CSU, and School Resource Officer
 Join a team dedicated to protecting our community while making a lasting impact in the lives of those who call #EastRidgeTN home.
 A City of East Ridge application is required to be considered for an open position. 
 📧 Please email your resume and cover letter to msinigaglio@eastridgetn.gov. 
 Note that if an application has not been submitted within the last 60 days, please reapply. 
 💻 Apply online today at:  www.eastridgetn.gov/human_resources.html
 The City of East Ridge is an Equal Opportunity Employer and a Tennessee Drug-Free Workplace.

items.title: 🌿 Make Your Yard RainSmart & Reduce Your Stormwater Fees! 💧 The City of Red Bank is proud to offer the RainSmart Yards...
items.content_text:
🌿 Make Your Yard RainSmart & Reduce Your Stormwater Fees! 💧
 The City of Red Bank is proud to offer the RainSmart Yards program — a simple way for homeowners to help reduce stormwater runoff and protect our local creeks and streams. By managing the rainwater on your property with practices like rain gardens, native plantings, and rain barrels, you can:
 ✅ Improve local water quality
 ✅ Reduce pollution flowing into Red Bank's stormwater system and waterways
 ✅ Create habitat for beneficial pollinators
 ✅ Help our community stay flood-resilient
 And here’s the great news:
 If your yard earns RainSmart certification, you can receive a stormwater utility fee discount from the City of Red Bank based on your rating:
 🌟 Bronze – 25% stormwater fee reduction
 🌟 Silver – 50% stormwater fee reduction
 🏆 A Certified yard = lower annual stormwater fee!
 Participation also gives you access to free workshops and resources to help you get started.
 Learn more and take the first step toward certification: mywaterways.org/rainsmart-yards
 Let’s work together to keep Red Bank’s waterways healthy — and save money while we do it! 🌎💙 
 #CleanWater #RainSmart #Stormwater

Some careers become family traditions.

For Ladd Ledford, Komatsu was a place to grow, learn new skills and build a career. Today, he's proud to share that experience with his son, Hayden, who represents the next generation of Komatsu employees.

There's still time to register for our FREE TEEN TECH GOES HOME COURSE! 
 Looking for a fun and productive way for your teen to spend part of the summer? The Tech Goes Home Tennessee program is coming to Jessie Igou Community Center!
 📍 Location: Jessie Igou Community Center
 📅 Dates: July 27–31, 2026
 ⏰ Time: 10:30 AM – 1:30 PM
 Teens will learn:
 ✅ Basic computer skills
 ✅ Internet safety
 ✅ Digital tools for school success
 ✅ Hands-on experience with real devices
 🎁 Bonus: Teens who successfully complete the program will receive a FREE laptop!
 📋 Parent consent and waiver are required. Space is limited, so register early!
 📞 For more information, call 423-643-6989 or stop by the center to register.
